suggestions: Task 8 is structurally broken: the description is truncated, test_strategy is empty, and there's a malformed 'D12' key at the task root level. Regenerate Task 8 with complete description, full details for all 5 subtasks, and a proper test_strategy covering all interactive components.

Task 3 (comprehensive integration test suite) substantially overlaps with Task 1 subtask 1008 and Task 2 subtask 2006 which already define 6 and 8 integration tests respectively. Either remove the smoke tests from Tasks 1/2 and consolidate all testing into Task 3, or narrow Task 3's scope to only the cross-cutting edge cases (Groups 8-12) not already covered, eliminating the architectural redundancy.

Task 4 subtask 4005 (controller reconciliation loop integration) spans receipt generation, S3 upload, quality determination, and Redis publication — these are four distinct concerns. Split into: (a) billing amount computation and quality determination helper, (b) receipt generation and S3 upload orchestration, (c) reconciliation loop hook wiring. This would enable parallel implementation and cleaner unit test boundaries.

Task 8's top-level description is truncated mid-sentence and its test_strategy field is empty — regenerate the complete description and add a comprehensive test_strategy covering all 5 subtasks (8001-8005) similar to the pattern used in Tasks 3, 6, and 7.

Tasks 3's subtasks 3002-3005 all depend only on 3001 and are marked parallelizable, but they test instructions from Tasks 1 and 2 — if the test infrastructure setup (3001) is done, all four test group subtasks can run in parallel, which is good. However, Task 3 itself blocks on both Tasks 1 AND 2, meaning the entire comprehensive test suite waits for settlement to be complete. Consider splitting Task 3 so Groups 1-4 (non-settlement tests) can start as soon as Task 1 finishes, unblocking earlier feedback.

The sidecar (Task 5) depends on Tasks 1, 2, AND 4, creating a 3-deep serial chain. Subtasks 5001, 5002, 5003, and 5005 have no actual code dependency on Task 4's implementation — they only need the SettlementEvent schema. Extract the shared event schema into a common crate or type definition that both Task 4 and Task 5 can consume independently, allowing sidecar development to start in parallel with the controller billing integration.

Eliminate Task 3 entirely — Tasks 1 and 2 already include comprehensive test suites (6 and 8 test cases respectively). A separate 30+ test integration task duplicates coverage and adds no value for a hackathon. Fold any unique edge cases (Groups 8-12) into Task 2's test subtask 2006.

Collapse Task 1's subtasks 1001, 1003, and 1004 (workspace init, utils module, error enum) into a single subtask — these are each 15-30 minutes of work and don't warrant independent tracking. Similarly, Task 7's 8 subtasks should be collapsed to 4-5 (merge 7001+7002, merge 7003+7007).

Task 8 has a truncated description, empty test_strategy, and a stray 'D12' field — this task needs to be regenerated. Additionally, the web dashboard (Tasks 7+8) and CLI demo (Task 6) both serve the same purpose (hackathon demo). Pick one and defer the other.
